# @poukai-inc/ui — design system rules for LLM consumers

This file is the authoritative brand contract for `@poukai-inc/ui`. Hand-authored by `poukai-design`. Takes precedence over inline comments, story descriptions, or any generated output (including `dist/llms.txt`). Read before writing, reviewing, or modifying any component, token, or style in this repository.

Consumed via: `cat node_modules/@poukai-inc/ui/dist/llms-full.txt`

---

## Token semantics

All tokens live in `src/tokens/tokens.css` on `:root`. Owned exclusively by `poukai-design`. Engineers read tokens; they never modify the token file. If an implementation requires a value with no token, work stops until `poukai-design` adds it.

### Color

- `--bg: #fbfbfd` — Page background (apple.com canvas). NEVER pure `#FFFFFF`; the off-white value gives elevation headroom above the page and allows clean dark-mode inversion. The only surfaces that should be `--bg` are the literal page canvas and elements that need to feel "on the page" (not elevated above it).
- `--bg-elevated: #ffffff` — Front-most elevation layer. Reserved exclusively for popovers, sheets, modals, and dialogs. This is the ONLY surface where pure `#FFFFFF` is permitted — it reads as "elevated above the page" precisely because the page is `#FBFBFD`. NEVER use `--bg-elevated` for page background or content sections.
- `--surface: #f5f5f7` — Recessed elevation (one step below `--bg`). Used for code blocks, quote backgrounds, card fills (RoleCard). NEVER use as body text. NEVER use as a section divider. `--surface` recedes below the page canvas; `--bg-elevated` rises above it.
- `--surface-section: #f8f8fa` — Page-section recessed background. Use for full-width alternating section bands on editorial pages only. Subtler than `--surface` (halfway between `--bg` and `--surface`). NEVER use for inline elements (code blocks, card fills) — use `--surface` for those. NEVER stack adjacent to `--surface` on the same page. Maximum 5 alternating bands per page.
- `--fg: #1d1d1f` — Primary text, wordmark fill, sigil stroke, and the `primary` button background (inverts: `--fg` fill, `--bg` text). The only color allowed for body paragraph text. NEVER use decoratively as a background wash.
- `--fg-muted: #6e6e73` — Secondary text: captions, footer copy, eyebrow labels, lede paragraphs, hiredBy lines, Stat provenance. NEVER use for primary calls to action. NEVER use as a disabled-state substitute.
- `--hairline: #d2d2d7` — Dividers, 1px rules, borders. Always applied as a border/border-top/border-bottom, NEVER as a background. Never render thicker than `--hairline-w: 1px`.
- `--accent: #0071e3` — The brand’s single accent. Roles: status dot fill, link underline-on-hover (the accent layer grows over the resting hairline underline), focus rings. NEVER use as a surface fill, body text color, or button background. It is a signal color: interactivity and focus only. `--accent` is NEVER visible in a resting state — it appears only on hover (animated underline) and on focus-visible (ring).
- `--accent-glow: rgba(0, 113, 227, 0.18)` — Transparent halo for the available status dot and `::selection`. NEVER use as a container fill or hover state. Never stack it.
- `--bg-warm-accent: #c0452c` — Editorial warm-accent band background (full-bleed orange). Restraint rules in `meta/brand.md`: at most one warm band per page; never adjacent to another `--bg-warm-accent` band. NEVER use for inline elements or component fills.
- `--fg-on-warm: #fdf5f0` — Primary text on warm bands. Warm-tinted near-white. The only text color permitted as primary copy over `--bg-warm-accent`. NEVER use over `--bg`.
- `--fg-on-warm-muted: #f5ddd0` — Supporting text on warm bands. Use sparingly; see `meta/brand.md` for the contrast ceiling. NEVER use as primary copy.

**Status color tier — product feedback only (NOT editorial brand).** Six tokens for error/warning state surfaces (Banner danger/warning tones, Form invalid borders, future Toast). Distinct from `--bg-warm-accent` (editorial brand orange) by hue: danger is true red, warning is amber/ochre.

- `--danger: #b3261e` — True red. Used by Form invalid border (`Input`/`Textarea`/`Field` `[aria-invalid="true"]`), Banner danger left-rule, future destructive button variant. WCAG AA on `--bg` (#FBFBFD): ~7.0:1. NEVER use as surface fill, body text, or non-error indicator.
- `--bg-danger: #fef0f0` — Tinted page-tier surface (subtle pink). Used by Banner danger background. NEVER use as primary page background; this is reserved for the danger-banner surface.
- `--fg-on-danger: #5c1310` — Deep oxblood for text on `--bg-danger` (~9.1:1). Use only on `--bg-danger` surfaces.
- `--warning: #b46100` — Amber/ochre. Used by Banner warning left-rule, future caution indicators. Distinct from `--bg-warm-accent` (#C0452C, brick-red editorial) — different hue family. WCAG AA on `--bg` (#FBFBFD): ~4.6:1.
- `--bg-warning: #fff7e6` — Tinted page-tier surface (light cream). Used by Banner warning background. NEVER substitute for `--bg-warm-accent`; the warm-accent band is editorial, this is product feedback.
- `--fg-on-warning: #4a2900` — Deep brown for text on `--bg-warning` (~10.2:1). Use only on `--bg-warning` surfaces.

**Elevation rhythm.** Four steps, low to high: `--surface (#F5F5F7) < --surface-section (#F8F8FA) < --bg (#FBFBFD) < --bg-elevated (#FFFFFF)`. Use `--surface` for recessed inline elements (code blocks, quote blocks, card fills). Use `--surface-section` for full-width alternating section bands on editorial pages — this is the only tier permitted as a section divider. Use `--bg` for the page canvas and page-level content. Use `--bg-elevated` for overlays that must read as front-most. Never skip steps. Never use `--surface` as a section divider.

**Never pure edges.** `--fg` is never `#000000`; `--bg` is never pure `#FFFFFF`. Both ends of the neutral ramp pull in slightly — this gives elevation headroom on the page and ensures the palette inverts cleanly to dark mode without losing its restraint. Treat any spec or hardcoded value that implies a pure edge as a brand-level escalation.

### Dark mode

Dark mode is implemented as a `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` block appended to `src/tokens/tokens.css`. It overrides every color token on `:root`. No component CSS changes — every component that consumes `var(--token)` references gains dark mode automatically. Full rationale in `meta/design/dark-mode.md`.

**Philosophy.** Dark mode is not an inversion of the light palette. It is a parallel surface stack: backgrounds darken progressively toward black (the floor); foregrounds brighten toward near-white (never pure). The elevation rhythm inverts direction — surfaces RISE through lighter grays as they elevate, rather than toward white. `html { color-scheme: light dark }` is declared so browser-native controls (scrollbars, form inputs, selection) match the token-driven scheme.

**Dark-mode token table.**

| Token | Dark value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `--bg` | `#000000` | Page floor. Apple canonical dark-mode canvas. |
| `--bg-elevated` | `#1C1C1E` | Popovers, sheets, dialogs. Apple iOS `systemBackground` dark. |
| `--surface` | `#1C1C1E` | Recessed inline blocks. Same raw value as `--bg-elevated`; semantically distinct by context. |
| `--surface-section` | `#161618` | Section bands. Slightly lighter than `--bg`, preserving the recessed-band rhythm. |
| `--fg` | `#F5F5F7` | Primary text. Near-white (10/255 off pure). Contrast on `--bg`: **19.58:1** (AAA). |
| `--fg-muted` | `#86868B` | Secondary text. Apple `secondaryLabel` dark. Contrast on `--bg`: **6.10:1** (AA). |
| `--hairline` | `#2C2C2E` | Dividers. Apple `separator` dark value. |
| `--accent` | `#0A84FF` | Links, focus rings. Apple `systemBlue` dark. Contrast on `--bg`: **5.86:1** (AA; ≥3:1 required for non-text). |
| `--accent-glow` | `rgba(10,132,255,0.24)` | Halo / selection. Higher opacity compensates for dark surface absorption. |
| `--bg-warm-accent` | `#8A2E1C` | Editorial band — dimmed to ~55% luminance of light value ("deep ember"). |
| `--fg-on-warm` | `#FDF5F0` | Unchanged. Contrast on dark `--bg-warm-accent`: **7.75:1** (AAA). |
| `--fg-on-warm-muted` | `#D6B9A8` | Contrast on dark `--bg-warm-accent`: **~4.8:1** (AA). |
| `--danger` | `#FF453A` | Apple `systemRed` dark. |
| `--bg-danger` | `#1C0A0A` | Near-black, red undertone. |
| `--fg-on-danger` | `#FFB4B0` | Contrast on `--bg-danger`: **11.63:1** (AAA). |
| `--warning` | `#FF9F0A` | Apple `systemOrange` dark. |
| `--bg-warning` | `#1C1408` | Near-black, amber undertone. |
| `--fg-on-warning` | `#FFD6A0` | Contrast on `--bg-warning`: **13.74:1** (AAA). |

**Contrast budget (all met).**

| Pair | Ratio | Threshold | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--fg` on `--bg` | 19.58:1 | 7:1 (AAA body) | AAA |
| `--fg-muted` on `--bg` | 6.10:1 | 4.5:1 (AA normal) | AA |
| `--accent` on `--bg` | 5.86:1 | 3:1 (non-text) | AA normal |
| `--fg-on-warm` on `--bg-warm-accent` | 7.75:1 | 4.5:1 | AAA |
| `--fg-on-warm-muted` on `--bg-warm-accent` | ~4.8:1 | 4.5:1 | AA |
| `--fg-on-danger` on `--bg-danger` | 11.63:1 | 4.5:1 | AAA |
| `--fg-on-warning` on `--bg-warning` | 13.74:1 | 4.5:1 | AAA |

**Component audit.** All shipped atoms, molecules, and organisms consume only `var(--token)` references and gain dark mode for free. One known exception: `Dialog.module.css` scrim (`rgb(0 0 0 / 0.4)`) is intentionally inline per its spec — acceptable in dark mode but flagged for future opacity review.

### Typography

- `--font-serif: "Instrument Serif", Georgia, …` — Editorial display face. Used for h1, h2, RoleCard titles, Principle titles, FailureMode titles, Stat numeral. NEVER use for UI chrome.
- `--font-sans: "Geist", -apple-system, …` — Primary UI face. All body copy, navigation, buttons, captions, badges, eyebrows. Self-hosted in src/tokens/fonts/. NEVER substitute another sans-serif.
- `--font-mono: "Geist Mono", ui-monospace, …` — Used for Stat source lines and RoleCard eyebrow labels. Encodes "reference" or "label" semantics.
- `--fs-tagline: clamp(2.25rem, 1.5rem + 3.5vw, 4.25rem)` (36-68px) — The h1 display scale. Used exactly once per page for the Hero title.
- `--fs-tagline-intimate: clamp(2rem, 1.25rem + 2.5vw, 3.25rem)` (32-52px) — Hero title at `size="intimate"`. Use only inside the Hero molecule. Never use directly on `h1` outside Hero context.
- `--fs-statement: clamp(1.75rem, 1.25rem + 2vw, 2.75rem)` (28-44px) — Italic-serif editorial Statement block. Sits between `h2` and `--fs-tagline-intimate`. Used only by the `<Statement>` molecule; not a heading replacement and does not emit any heading element.
- `--fs-pull: clamp(1.25rem, 1rem + 1vw, 1.625rem)` (20-26px) — Inline editorial pull-quote accent. Above `--fs-body`, below `--fs-statement`. Used only by the `<Pull>` molecule. Fills the gap between body and Statement; a distinct semantic rung with reuse potential for future inline callout components. No `size` variant — one scale carries.
- `--fs-body: clamp(1.0625rem, 1rem + 0.3vw, 1.1875rem)` (17-19px) — Primary reading size.
- `--fs-meta: 0.875rem` (14px) — Secondary text: captions, nav labels, badge text, hiredBy lines.
- `--fs-micro: 0.75rem` (12px) — Uppercase labels only. Always paired with letter-spacing: 0.04em and text-transform: uppercase.
- `--fs-stat: clamp(2.75rem, 2rem + 3vw, 4.5rem)` (44-72px) — Stat numeral at size="md".
- `--fs-stat-large: clamp(3.5rem, 2.25rem + 5vw, 6rem)` (56-96px) — Stat numeral at size="lg". One per section at most.
- `--tracking-stat: -0.015em` — Negative letter-spacing for display numerals in Stat only.
- `--tracking-micro: 0.04em` — Canonical tracking for micro/uppercase labels. Used by `.micro` global class, `Stat` source line, and `Hero` eyebrow (variant="no-title"). Always paired with `text-transform: uppercase` and `--fs-micro`.
- `--tracking-eyebrow: 0.06em` — Canonical letter-spacing for all Eyebrow labels. Used by `<Eyebrow>`, `RoleCard` eyebrow, `FieldNote` label. At `--fs-meta` (14px) = 0.84px per letter — enough to communicate uppercase structure without tipping airy.
- `--tracking-numeric: 0.08em` — Wider tracking for sequential numeric markers. Used by `FailureMode` index (`.index`). Slightly wider than eyebrow tracking to visually separate reference labels from editorial labels.
- `--lh-body: 1.55` — Canonical body line-height. Applied to global `body`, `RoleCard` body, `Statement` supporting, `FieldNote` body, `LinkCard` body, `FeatureCard` body, `TeamCard` bio, `Dialog` description and body. The default body reading rhythm.
- `--lh-body-relaxed: 1.6` — Relaxed body line-height for longer-form editorial prose. Applied to `Principle` content and `FailureMode` body — intentionally looser than `--lh-body` to give the longer editorial paragraphs more air. Do not use for UI chrome or short card copy.
- `--lh-meta: 1.2` — Line-height for meta/eyebrow-scale text. First named line-height token in the system, scoped to the meta register. Matches the value already used on `h2`, `h3`, and `.numeral` throughout the token file.
- `--fs-card-title: clamp(1.5rem, 1.15rem + 1.2vw, 2rem)` (24-32px) — Card-title rung. Shared by `<RoleCard>`, `<Principle>`, `<FailureMode>` titles. Always rendered in `--font-serif`, `font-weight: 400`. Do NOT use as a heading-level replacement; the title element semantics still come from the consuming page.

### Spacing (4px base grid)

- `--space-1`: 0.25rem (4px) — Tight: icon-to-label gap
- `--space-2`: 0.5rem (8px) — Inline gap: eyebrow-to-title
- `--space-3`: 0.75rem (12px) — Badge dot-to-text, Stat column gap
- `--space-4`: 1rem (16px) — Body paragraph margin, card internal rhythm
- `--space-6`: 1.5rem (24px) — Hero status-to-title, nav gap, card padding tier
- `--space-8`: 2rem (32px) — Hero title-to-lede, Principle padding
- `--space-10`: 2.5rem (40px) — Editorial bump above `--space-8` (h1 desktop bottom margin, Hero CTA desktop top margin, FailureMode top padding)
- `--space-12`: 3rem (48px) — Principle desktop padding, main content top
- `--space-16`: 4rem (64px) — Large section gaps
- `--space-24`: 6rem (96px) — Main content bottom padding
- `--space-32`: 8rem (128px) — Maximum editorial breathing room

Other gaps in the scale (--space-5, --space-7, --space-9, --space-11, etc.) do not exist. NEVER use raw pixel values.

### Layout

- `--page-pad: clamp(1.5rem, 2vw + 1rem, 3rem)` — Horizontal padding for SiteShell header/footer. The only token that governs the outer page edge.
- `--content-max: 64rem` (1024px) — Maximum width of the main content column. NEVER exceed in the main column layout.
- `--hero-max: 38rem` (608px) — Maximum width of the Hero text column. Applies at all widths. NEVER override with a wider value.
- `--hero-illustration-max: 25rem` (400px) — Maximum width of the Hero illustration column when the `illustration` slot is active. Applies above the 720px breakpoint; the illustration column is hidden below that.

### Motion

- `--easing: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` — Expo-out for entrance animations.
- `--easing-link: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1)` — Link underline grow animation.
- `--dur-press: 80ms` — Tactile click/press feedback (button `:active` transform, immediate-response interactions). Deliberately shorter than `--dur-fast` so the press lands without lag.
- `--dur-fast: 180ms` — Micro-interactions.
- `--dur-mid: 240ms` — Standard transition.
- `--dur-slow: 600ms` — Entrance animations.
- `--dur-pulse: 1800ms` — `<StatusBadge status="available">` halo pulse (intentionally slow + meditative, paired with `ease-out`).

All motion is gated by `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` via a `:root !important` block. This block is the ONLY permitted use of `!important` in the codebase.

### Radii and rules

- `--radius-1: 2px` — Focus rings only.
- `--radius-2: 4px` — Button corners and the icon well in RoleCard.
- `--radius-3: 8px` — RoleCard container.
- `--hairline-w: 1px` — Universal border width. NEVER use 2px or more for decorative borders.

### Button height ladder

The `<Button>` height ladder is part of the brand contract. Four named rungs, hand-tuned, not interpolated.

- `--btn-h-sm: 32px` — `size="sm"` min-height. Minor inline action.
- `--btn-h-compact: 40px` — `size="compact"` min-height. Editorial CTA — quieter than the page default. Apple's "Compact" rung.
- `--btn-h-md: 44px` — `size="md"` min-height. Page-default CTA. The only rung that passes WCAG 2.5.5 AAA at threshold.
- `--btn-h-lg: 52px` — `size="lg"` min-height. Loud CTA.

Padding and font-size stay hand-tuned per size (no separate padding-token scale). Consumed by `src/atoms/Button/Button.module.css`; available to any future primitive (form fields, segmented controls) that needs to align to the same height ladder.

---

## Component recommended usages

### Wordmark
- Min height: 40px. SiteShell default is height={56}.
- Aspect ratio locked at ~4.08:1 (viewBox 0 0 1184 290). Set only `height`; width is auto.
- Do NOT render below height={40}. Do NOT add a second Wordmark inside SiteShell's children. Do NOT use as a watermark. Do NOT set label="" (it is the a11y name).
- Path geometry changes require Arian's approval (ADR-0011).

### StatusBadge
- Maximum 1 instance per page. Encodes consultant/service availability — not system status.
- status="available" triggers pulse animation automatically. Do NOT add additional CSS animation.
- prefers-reduced-motion disables the pulse at the CSS level — do NOT force-animate via JS or inline styles.
- Children: single short clause, 10 words or fewer.

### Button
- Maximum one variant="primary" per visual section.
- Labels always sentence-case, never ALL CAPS.
- Min heights: size="sm" 32px, size="compact" 40px, size="md" 44px, size="lg" 52px. Default is size="md".
- size="compact" is the editorial CTA — quieter than md, louder than sm. WCAG 2.5.5 AAA tap target (44px) requires md or lg; sm and compact are AA tiers.
- Use asChild with an <a> element for link-styled buttons. Do NOT nest a <button> inside Button with asChild.

### Stat
- Requires a caption — numeral alone is not self-explanatory.
- value must be a numeral or numeral+unit string. source should be citation only.
- Maximum 4 Stats per section. Maximum 1 size="lg" per section.

### Hero
- One per page. Do NOT nest Hero inside another Hero.
- status slot: StatusBadge or equivalent only (default variant only; not available in no-title variant).
- lede: 1-3 sentences at most.
- align="center": standalone landing contexts only.
- size="display" (default): title uses `--fs-tagline` (36-68px). Default for all product, marketing, and feature pages.
- size="intimate": title uses `--fs-tagline-intimate` (32-52px). Opt-in for low-density doorways (homepages, principle lists) where a quieter register suits the context. All other Hero rhythm (gaps, lede, CTA, color, font family, em accent) is unchanged.
- variant="no-title": renders eyebrow + lede only — no heading element of any level. Use only when the page's `<h1>` lives in body content. The molecule does not emit an `<h1>`; the consumer must. Eyebrow is rendered at `--fs-micro` (12px), uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.04em`, `--fg-muted`. The `title` and `status` props are excluded from this variant's type signature. `align`, `entrance`, and `cta` remain available.
- illustration: optional ReactNode slot (added 0.15.0). When provided, Hero switches to a two-column layout at `--bp-md` (768px) and above (text left, illustration right); the illustration column is hidden below 768px (stacked layout: illustration above the title block). Max width of the illustration column is controlled by `--hero-illustration-max` (25rem). Slot is content-agnostic — accepts SVG, `<img>`, `<Portrait>`, or any ReactNode. When `entrance="stagger"` is active, the illustration is the fifth stagger slot (index 4, 600ms delay). If decorative, the consumer must pass `aria-hidden="true"` or use `alt=""` on inner imagery. The Hero molecule does not enforce decorative-vs-meaningful semantics on the slot — the consumer owns that decision.

### RoleCard
- Eyebrow format: "Role 01", "Role 02" — Title Case "Role", zero-padded two-digit numeral.
- hiredBy footer: names separated by " · " (middle dot with spaces).
- Min 2 cards per use; max 6 per grid.

### Principle
- Numeral convention: lowercase Roman numerals with trailing period ("i.", "ii.", "iii.").
- Numeral is aria-hidden. Min 2 Principles per use.
- Distinct from `FailureMode`: `Principle.numeral` uses Roman numerals (i, ii, iii) for editorial / philosophical sequencing. The two components encode different semantic registers — do not conflate or rename either prop to match the other.

### FailureMode
- index is typed number, auto-formats as zero-padded ("01"). Do NOT pass as string.
- Index span is aria-hidden. Titles should be declarative noun phrases.
- Distinct from `Principle`: `FailureMode.index` + `indexLabel` use Arabic-numeral integer indices with optional formatted labels (e.g. "FM-01") for enumerated failure cases. The two components encode different semantic registers — do not conflate or rename either prop to match the other.

### Statement
- Editorial italic-serif block at `--fs-statement` (28-44px), `line-height: 1.2`, `letter-spacing: -0.005em`, `text-wrap: balance`. Not a heading element; emits no `<h1>`–`<h6>`.
- `statement` prop is required ReactNode, rendered inside a `<p>`. `supporting` is an optional second `<p>` at `--fs-body` in `--fg-muted`.
- `as="p"` (default) renders the root as a `<div>` wrapping the two paragraphs. `as="blockquote"` renders the root as `<blockquote>` — use this only when quoting an attributed external source.
- `hairline={true}` adds a decorative 1px top rule plus `--space-12` padding-top. Default `false`.
- Use sparingly: an editorial statement, not a tagline replacement. The page `<h1>` still lives in `<Hero>`.

### Portrait
- Editorial photography primitive. Renders `<picture>` with AVIF > WebP > JPEG fallback chain and srcset at 4 widths derived from the canonical `width`.
- `alt` is required and must be non-empty. Dev-mode throws on empty alt — portraits are never decorative (WCAG 1.1.1). Do NOT pass `alt=""`.
- `aspect` (`1:1` | `3:4` | `4:3` | `16:9` | `9:16`) drives the CSS `aspect-ratio` and the computed `height` attribute. Locked at render time to prevent CLS.
- `width` is the canonical source-image width in px — drives the srcset divisors (1, 1.5, 2.34375, 3.75) and the `width`/`height` HTML attrs. Pass the full source resolution, not the display size.
- `loading` defaults to `"lazy"`; use `"eager"` for above-the-fold portraits. `fetchPriority` defaults to `"auto"`; pair `loading="eager"` with `fetchPriority="high"` for the LCP image. `sizes` defaults to `"100vw"`. `objectPosition` defaults to `"center"`.
- `src` accepts either a string URL or an object shaped like Astro's `ImageMetadata` (`.src` is extracted). Do NOT pass `import.meta` imports without unwrapping.

### Eyebrow
- Canonical micro-label atom. Uppercase, tracked (`--tracking-eyebrow: 0.06em`), `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta` (14px), weight 500, `--lh-meta` (1.2).
- `variant="muted"` (default) — color `--fg-muted`. Standard section/category/role label register.
- `variant="solid"` — color `--fg`. Full contrast when the label needs more weight.
- `variant="numbered"` — color `--fg-muted` + activates the inline numeral slot (inline-flex, baseline-aligned, `--space-2` gap). Use with `numeral` prop.
- `numeral` prop (string, optional) — rendered in a leading `<span>` with `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`. e.g. `numeral="FM-03"`, `numeral="01 ·"`.
- `as` prop — polymorphic root element, defaults to `<span>`. Valid values: `span`, `p`, `div`, `dt`, `h2`, `h3`, `h4`, `h5`, `h6`, `li`. Use `as="h2"` when heading semantics are required at eyebrow scale.
- `margin: 0` — Eyebrow owns no margin. The consuming molecule owns spacing between Eyebrow and its sibling title.
- Resolves the drift between `RoleCard.eyebrow` (mono, 12px, 0.06em), `FailureMode.index` (sans, 14px, 0.08em), and `.micro` utility (sans, 12px, 0.04em). Migration to consuming molecules is a follow-up PR.
- Do NOT use `--accent` on Eyebrow — it is a label, not a link or status indicator.
- Do NOT add truncation or overflow clipping — the DS does not manage Eyebrow overflow.

### EmailLink
- Canonical `mailto:` affordance atom. Not a button — it opens a mail client, it does not commit to an outcome. Choose `<EmailLink>` over `<Button asChild><a href="mailto:…">` when the contact register is "reach out" rather than "commit to action."
- `email` (required string) — The email address. Component always computes `href="mailto:${email}"`. Consumers NEVER pass `href` directly — it is excluded from the prop interface via `Omit<AnchorHTMLAttributes, 'href'>`.
- `label` (optional string) — Visible link text. Defaults to the `email` string when omitted. When provided, `label` is the accessible name; the email is the href target only.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode) — Leading icon slot. When present, root shifts to `inline-flex` with `align-items: center` and `gap: --space-2`. When absent, root is a plain inline anchor. Use 14px icons in `--fs-meta` contexts, 16px in `--fs-body` contexts. Pass `aria-hidden="true"` on decorative icons.
- `qualifier` (optional string) — Trailing role qualifier rendered as ` (qualifier)` in a muted `<span>` inside the anchor. Pass the bare name/role without parentheses. e.g. `qualifier="Arian"` renders "founder@pouk.ai (Arian)".
- `variant="default"` (default) — `color: --fg`, hover → `--accent`. Use on `--bg` or `--surface` contexts.
- `variant="muted"` — `color: --fg-muted`, hover → `--fg`. For footer, caption, secondary-copy contexts. Matches existing `.muted-link` treatment in `SiteShell`.
- Underline inherits the global `a` two-layer underline rule in `tokens.css`. At rest: a 1px `--hairline` underline is always visible (resting discoverability signal). On hover: a 1px `--accent` underline grows left-to-right over the hairline layer via `background-size` transition (`--dur-mid`, `--easing-link`). `<EmailLink>` is a link — same affordance as every other navigational anchor. No underline override.
- Focus ring: `outline: 2px solid --accent`, `outline-offset: 4px` (link convention — matches global `a:focus-visible`, NOT Button's 2px offset). `border-radius: --radius-1`.
- `:visited` — no distinct treatment (brand decision; visited state creates false scent on contact links).
- `:active` — no `translateY` (link, not button).
- No margin on root — consumer owns spacing.
- Do NOT use `<EmailLink>` for `tel:` or `sms:` protocols — those get their own atoms.
- Do NOT compose `<EmailLink>` inside `<Button>` — they are sibling affordances with different registers.
- Do NOT pass `href` — it is always computed from `email`. The prop is excluded at the TypeScript level.

### Tag
- Inline categorical pill atom. Communicates type, category, topic, or metadata classification of adjacent content. Answers "what kind of thing is this?" inline with content flow: inside a card, in a topic list, beside a title, or inside a sentence.
- Root element: `<span>`. Non-polymorphic — no `as` prop. Always `<span>`. Tags are inline categorical labels; they do not need heading, list-item, or block semantics.
- `children` (required ReactNode) — the visible label text. Typically a plain string (one to three words). ReactNode accepted for rare inline `<strong>` emphasis, but a plain string is the idiomatic usage.
- `tone` (`"default"` | `"muted"`, default `"default"`) — visual tone. Two tones only; no colored semantic tones.
  - `"default"` — `background-color: var(--surface)` (#F5F5F7), `color: var(--fg)` (#1D1D1F), no border. Primary categorical register. Use in cards and topic lists.
  - `"muted"` — `background-color: transparent`, `color: var(--fg-muted)` (#6E6E73), `border: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)` (1px #D2D2D7). Secondary or receding register. Use for draft labels, optional tags, or where the tag must not compete with primary content.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode) — optional leading icon slot. When present, root shifts from `display: inline` to `display: inline-flex` with `align-items: center` and `gap: var(--space-2)` (8px). When absent, root is plain `display: inline`. Recommended icon size: 12px (proportional inside a 14px-text pill). Pass `aria-hidden="true"` on all decorative icons — the DS does not enforce this at the type level, but it is the required consumer contract.
- Typography: `--font-sans` (Geist), `--fs-meta` (14px fixed), `font-weight: 400`, `line-height: var(--lh-meta)` (1.2), `letter-spacing: normal` (no tracking — Tags are mixed-case; tracking is for uppercase labels like Eyebrow).
- Geometry: `border-radius: 999px` (pill — a geometric constant, not a brand token; documented in Tag.module.css). `padding-block: var(--space-1)` (4px). `padding-inline: var(--space-2)` (8px). `box-sizing: border-box` — ensures muted tone's 1px border is absorbed inside the padding box; pill dimensions are identical across both tones.
- `white-space: nowrap` — Tags never wrap internally. If a list of Tags wraps at narrow viewports, the parent flex container owns that.
- No states, no motion, no transitions. Tag is a non-interactive static label. No `:hover`, `:focus`, `:active`, or `[disabled]` rules.
- Tokens consumed: `--fs-meta`, `--font-sans`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--surface`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--lh-meta`. No new tokens introduced.
- Contrast: `"default"` tone `--fg` on `--surface` = 15.46:1 (AAA). `"muted"` tone `--fg-muted` on `--bg` = 4.91:1 (AA normal at 14px — passes WCAG 2.1 4.5:1 threshold). Muted border is decorative; no contrast requirement under §1.4.11.
- Composition: use in any inline or flex context. Multiple Tags in a row: parent flex container with `gap: var(--space-2)` and `flex-wrap: wrap`. Inside prose `<p>`: natural inline flow. No self-margin — consumer owns spacing.
- Tag is NOT `<Eyebrow>` (Eyebrow is block-level, uppercase, tracked, always above content). Tag is NOT `<StatusBadge>` (StatusBadge encodes liveness state with a colored dot). Tag is NOT a button (no onClick, no hover/focus states authored by the DS).
- Do NOT add colored semantic tones (`"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"accent"`). Any future colored tone requires a brand-level decision-log entry and Arian's explicit sign-off.
- Do NOT add a close button, `onRemove`, or `onClick` — that is a future `TagButton` primitive with its own spec.
- Do NOT make Tag polymorphic. The root is always `<span>`.
- Do NOT use Tag to signal liveness — use `<StatusBadge>`.
- Do NOT use Tag as a heading or section label — use `<Eyebrow>`.
- Do NOT add `text-overflow: ellipsis` or truncation. Tags are always short strings by design.

### Section
- Canonical page-section wrapper molecule. Owns the vertical rhythm around a section's header block (eyebrow + title + lede) and exposes a children slot for section body content.
- No visual surface — no background, border, or shadow in default configuration. Structural scaffolding only.
- `as` prop (default `"section"`) — polymorphic root element. Valid values: `"section"` | `"article"` | `"aside"` | `"div"`. `"section"` is a region landmark when titled; `"article"` for independently distributable content; `"aside"` for tangential content; `"div"` when a landmark would be noise.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) — String values are automatically wrapped in `<Eyebrow>` with default props (`variant="muted"`). ReactNode values are rendered as-is. Never wraps a ReactNode in a second Eyebrow. Always routes through the `<Eyebrow>` atom for consistency.
- `title` (optional `ReactNode`) — rendered as the element named by `titleAs`. When present, wires `aria-labelledby` on the root for landmark elements.
- `titleAs` (optional `"h1" | "h2" | "h3"`, default `"h2"`) — heading level for the title element. Default is `h2` (page subdivisions below a Hero which owns the h1). Pass `"h1"` when Section is at the top of a page without a Hero; `"h3"` for nested subdivision contexts.
- `lede` (optional `ReactNode`) — supporting copy below the title. String values are wrapped in `<p className="lede">` (applies `color: var(--fg-muted)` and `max-width: 36rem` via the existing `.lede` class in `tokens.css`). ReactNode values are rendered as-is.
- `size` (`"default"` | `"tight"`, default `"default"`) — block padding variant. `"default"` = `--space-16` (64px) top + bottom; `"tight"` = `--space-12` (48px) top + bottom.
- `children` — section body content. Section applies no styling to children — no max-width, no typography. The body is the consumer's space.
- **Empty header guard**: if `eyebrow`, `title`, and `lede` are all absent, the header wrapper div is not rendered.
- **`aria-labelledby` wiring**: when `title` is present and `as` is a landmark element (`"section"` / `"article"` / `"aside"`), the title element receives an auto-generated `id` (via `useId()`) and the root receives `aria-labelledby`. Skipped for `as="div"`.
- Header block max-width: `var(--hero-max)` (38rem). Children slot has no max-width.
- Header gaps: eyebrow→title `--space-2` (8px); title→lede `--space-4` (16px). Header→children gap: `--space-12` (48px).
- Global `h2` bottom margin is zeroed in Section's CSS scope (`margin: 0` on `.title`) — Section's flex layout controls spacing instead.
- No `bleed` prop. No `align` prop. Both excluded from spec — structural wrapper, not visual band.
- Do NOT nest Section inside Section. Would double block padding and break vertical rhythm.
- Do NOT use Section as a card, callout, or decorative container. It carries no visual surface.
- Do NOT add `aria-labelledby` manually when a title is provided — Section wires it automatically for landmark elements.

### Pull
- Inline editorial pull-quote molecule. Lives inside long-form prose between paragraphs. NOT a page-level molecule (unlike Hero or Statement).
- Root element: `<blockquote>` by default (correct HTML for quoted material). Use `as="aside"` only when Pull is an extracted editorial accent that is not a literal quotation from an external source.
- `variant="serif"` (default) — Instrument Serif italic at `--fs-pull` (20–26px fluid). Use for editorial long-form content, article pages, customer stories.
- `variant="sans"` — Geist roman at `--fs-pull`. Use for technical or operator-grade content where Instrument Serif feels mismatched.
- `--fs-pull: clamp(1.25rem, 1rem + 1vw, 1.625rem)` (20–26px) — the only scale. No `size` prop. A `large` variant was explicitly rejected; the page-level editorial scale is `<Statement>`.
- Left rule: `border-inline-start: 3px solid var(--hairline)`. 3px is a brand-locked constant (not `--hairline-w`). Decorative typographic punctuation, not a layout divider.
- `padding-inline-start: var(--space-4)` (16px) — distance from rule to body text.
- `margin-block: var(--space-8)` (32px) — breathing room above + below. Adjacent `<p>` margin-bottom adds 48px total gap (non-collapsing). This is correct editorial rhythm at --fs-pull scale.
- `max-width: var(--hero-max)` (38rem) — matches the article text column. Pull never exceeds prose measure.
- `cite` prop — URL mapped to HTML `cite` attribute on `<blockquote>`. Machine-readable only. SILENTLY IGNORED when `as="aside"` (not a valid attribute on aside).
- `attribution` prop (optional ReactNode) — visible trailing attribution line. Renders as `<footer>` inside `<blockquote>` (per HTML5 spec; scoped, not contentinfo landmark). Renders as `<p>` inside `<aside>`. Always `--fs-meta`, `--fg-muted`, italic regardless of variant.
- No decorative `"` quote glyph — the left rule is the typographic punctuation.
- Do NOT place Pull inside a `<Hero>`. Do NOT stack Pull with `<Statement>` on the same surface. Do NOT use Pull for person identity attribution — use `<Quote>` (once shipped).
- Multiple Pulls per page are permitted. More than 3 is an editorial judgment, not a DS constraint.
- Screen reader: `<blockquote>` is announced as a quotation; `<aside>` adds a complementary landmark — avoid `as="aside"` in articles with multiple Pulls (landmark noise).

### LinkCard
- Canonical interactive card primitive. The entire card surface is a single click target (`<a>` root). Use for navigational index pages (`/work`, `/posts`, `/case-studies`) where each grid item routes to a destination.
- Root element: `<a>` by default. When `asChild={true}`, root becomes a Radix `Slot` — pass a framework router `<Link>` as the child. Identical to the `Button` `asChild` pattern.
- `href` is required when `asChild` is false. A runtime `console.error` fires in development if `href` is absent and `asChild` is false.
- Do NOT pass both `href` and `asChild={true}`. When `asChild` is true the child element owns the href.
- `variant="default"` (default) — `--surface` background, `1px solid --hairline` border, `--radius-3` (8px) corners, `--space-8` (32px) inset padding all sides. Use in grid contexts.
- `variant="quiet"` — `--bg` background (no fill), hairline rule top only (`border-top: 1px solid --hairline`), no radius, padding-block `--space-4`, padding-inline `0`. Use in dense vertical list contexts.
- Hover: `border-color` shifts to `--accent`. Transition: `border-color var(--dur-mid) var(--easing-link)`. For quiet variant, `border-top-color` shifts.
- `:focus-visible`: `outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 4px`. Default variant adds `border-radius: --radius-3` on the outline to match card shape. Quiet variant: `border-radius: 0`.
- `:active`: `transform: translateY(1px)` at `--dur-press` (80ms). Both variants. Tactile press response matching `<Button>`.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) — String → auto-wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode → pass-through (consumer owns configuration). Same discriminant as `<Section>`: `typeof eyebrow === 'string'`.
- `title` (required `ReactNode`) — primary content and accessible name anchor. Renders as `titleAs` element. Typography: `--fs-card-title` (24–32px fluid), `--font-sans`, weight 500, `line-height: 1.3`, `letter-spacing: -0.005em`. Margin zeroed inside LinkCard flex scope (overrides global h3 margin).
- `titleAs` (`"h2" | "h3" | "h4"`, default `"h3"`) — heading element. Default `h3` is correct when page has `<h1>` (Hero) and `<h2>` (Section above the grid). Pass `"h2"` when no preceding h2 exists.
- `body` (optional `ReactNode`) — string → wrapped in `<p>`; ReactNode → pass-through. `--fs-body`, `--fg-muted`, `line-height: 1.55`.
- `footer` (optional `ReactNode`) — pushed to card bottom via flex spacer (`flex: 1`). Defaults to `--fg-muted` color, `--fs-meta` font size. ReactNode pass-through; consumer content can override.
- `icon` (optional `ReactNode`) — absolutely positioned at `top: --space-8; right: --space-8` within card padding box. `pointer-events: none`. Consumer-supplied. Conventional: `<ArrowUpRight size={16} />` from `lucide-react` for external links.
- `external` — when true: adds `target="_blank"`, `rel="noopener noreferrer"`, and appends a visually-hidden `<span>(opens in new tab)</span>` for screen readers. Does NOT auto-inject an icon — pass via `icon` slot.
- Global `<a>` underline animation (`background-image` grow) is suppressed on the card root via `background-image: none; padding-bottom: 0`. The card's sole hover signal is the border-color shift.
- **No nested interactive elements.** A `<button>` or `<a>` inside a LinkCard is a WCAG failure (nested interactivity). Footer tags and inline labels must be non-interactive (`<span>`, plain text). This is documented as a hard constraint, not a component-level enforcement.
- Accessible name is derived from text content — eyebrow + title + body all contribute. Body copy should be meaningful, not boilerplate ("Click to learn more" produces a poor accessible name).
- In a CSS Grid context: set `align-items: stretch` on the grid container and `height: 100%` on the LinkCard root so all cards in a row share the same height. The footer flex spacer then aligns footers to the same baseline.
- Do NOT mix `variant="default"` and `variant="quiet"` in the same grid — incoherent visual rhythm.
- Do NOT put `<Button>` or any interactive element inside a LinkCard.
- Do NOT use LinkCard for non-navigational content. Use `RoleCard` (structural, no click target) for role descriptions.

### TeamCard
- Canonical person tile molecule. Renders a single team member as a brand object: portrait + name + role + optional bio + optional contact affordance. Operator-grade register — practitioner credential, not a social media card.
- Root element: `<article>` by default (correct HTML for a self-contained, independently distributable content item). Polymorphic via `as` prop: `"article"` | `"section"` | `"div"`.
- `aria-labelledby` is wired automatically from the root landmark element to the name heading's `id` (via `useId()`). Skipped when `as="div"` — a `<div>` carries no landmark role.
- `portrait` (required `ReactNode`) — pass a fully-configured `<Portrait>` instance. TeamCard does NOT accept `portraitSrc` — Portrait's API is authoritative for `src`, `alt`, `aspect`, `width`, and `sizes`. Never pass a raw `<img>`.
- `name` (required `ReactNode`) — person's name. Rendered as the element specified by `nameAs`. Default `nameAs="h3"` is correct inside a `<Section>` (h2) on a page with a Hero (h1). Override to `"h2"` for standalone person pages; `"h4"` for deeply nested contexts.
- `role` (required `ReactNode`) — short role/title descriptor. Rendered as `<p>` at `--fs-meta` (14px), `color: var(--fg-muted)`. No uppercase treatment — role is read at prose speed.
- `bio` (optional `ReactNode`) — supporting prose paragraph. Rendered as `<p>` at `--fs-body`, `color: var(--fg)`, `line-height: 1.55`. When absent, no empty element is emitted.
- `contact` (optional `ReactNode`) — any contact affordance. Standard usage: `<EmailLink email="…" variant="muted" />`. Slot is untyped — LinkedIn links, GitHub handles, etc. work without spec changes. TeamCard provides no interaction states; the inner component owns them.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) — string values are automatically wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode values are rendered as-is (escape hatch). Never double-wraps a ReactNode. In grids, prefer placing the eyebrow in the Section header rather than repeating it on every card.
- `layout` (`"stacked"` | `"horizontal"`, default `"stacked"`) — portrait-text spatial relationship. `"stacked"`: portrait above, text below (grid-context default). `"horizontal"`: portrait left at fixed `5rem` width, text block right with `--space-6` gap; collapses to stacked below 768px.
- **No padding, no border, no background.** TeamCard is a content tile, not a chrome tile. The consumer's grid owns the column gutter. If a surface variant is needed, use `className` override at the consumer layer — it is not a DS variant.
- Gap stack (stacked layout): eyebrow→portrait `--space-3` (12px), portrait→name `--space-4` (16px), name→role `--space-2` (8px), role→bio `--space-4` (16px), bio→contact `--space-8` (32px), role→contact (no bio) `--space-8` (32px).
- Contrast: name heading `--fg` on `--bg` = 16.29:1 (AAA). Role `--fg-muted` on `--bg` = 4.91:1 (AA normal at 14px). Bio `--fg` on `--bg` = 16.29:1 (AAA).
- Do NOT pass `portraitSrc` — the prop does not exist. Portrait's API is authoritative.
- Do NOT make TeamCard interactive (`href`, `onClick`). It is not a link. Consumer wraps in `<a>` or `<Link>` if needed and owns that interaction pattern.
- Do NOT nest TeamCard inside Hero. Hero is the page h1 moment; heading-level conflict results.
- Do NOT compose TeamCard with RoleCard. They are sibling molecules with distinct semantics (person vs. function).

### FeatureCard
- Canonical structural feature-grid tile. Use for capability, service, or feature grids where items are peers (not a numbered sequence) and none of the tiles are navigational destinations.
- `as` prop (default `"article"`) — polymorphic root element. Valid values: `"article"` | `"section"` | `"div"` | `"li"`. Use `"li"` inside a `<ul>` feature list; `"section"` when region landmark semantics are required; `"div"` when landmark semantics would be noise. Consumer is responsible for wrapping `as="li"` FeatureCards in a `<ul>`.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode) — decorative icon. FeatureCard wraps it in `<span aria-hidden="true">`. Sets `color: currentColor` on the wrapper so the icon inherits `--fg`. Recommended icon sizes: `size={24}` or `size={32}` from lucide-react. Consumer still passes `aria-hidden="true"` on the icon node for clarity, but the wrapper guarantees screen readers skip it regardless.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) — String → auto-wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode → rendered as-is (no double-wrap). Same string-convention as Section, LinkCard, and TeamCard.
- `title` (required ReactNode) — feature name. Rendered as the element named by `titleAs`. `"h1"` is intentionally excluded — a feature tile is never a page heading.
- `titleAs` (optional `"h2" | "h3" | "h4"`, default `"h3"`) — heading level. `"h3"` is correct for a grid of tiles under a `<Section>` (h2) on a page with a `<Hero>` (h1).
- `body` (required ReactNode) — feature description. String → wrapped in `<p style="margin:0">` (suppresses global `p` bottom-margin double-stack inside flex column). ReactNode → rendered as-is.
- `footer` (optional ReactNode) — rendered inside a wrapper div with default `color: var(--fg-muted)` and `font-size: var(--fs-meta)`. When absent, no element is emitted and no gap is created.
- `variant` (`"default"` | `"bordered"`, default `"default"`) — `"default"` is transparent, no border, no radius. `"bordered"` adds `--surface` background, `1px solid --hairline` border, `--radius-3` corners.
- Padding: `--space-6` (24px) on all four sides in both variants — single value prevents layout shift when switching variants at runtime.
- Gap stack: icon→eyebrow/title `--space-6` (24px); eyebrow→title `--space-2` (8px); title→body `--space-3` (12px); body→footer `--space-6` (24px).
- `aria-labelledby` wired on `article` and `section` roots to the title's auto-generated id (via `useId()`). Omitted for `div` and `li` — neither carries a landmark role.
- No internal flex spacer. Footer alignment in a grid requires composition: `align-items: stretch` on the grid, `height: 100%` on the card root, `margin-top: auto` on the footer wrapper in consumer surface CSS.
- No states, no hover, no interactivity. FeatureCard is structural only. For an interactive tile, use `<LinkCard>`.
- Do NOT mix `default` and `bordered` variants in the same grid — incoherent visual rhythm.
- Do NOT use FeatureCard for navigational tiles — use `<LinkCard>`.
- Do NOT use FeatureCard for sequenced/numbered items — use `<Principle>` or `<FailureMode>`.
- Do NOT pass `titleAs="h1"` — a feature tile is never a page heading. The prop type excludes it.
- Do NOT compose FeatureCard with RoleCard in the same grid. RoleCard carries sequencing semantics that conflict with FeatureCard's peer-item register.

### FieldNote
- Inline technical-aside molecule. A short parenthetical callout — a sentence or two — that sits inline with body copy and provides a factual clarification, caveat, or data footnote without interrupting reading flow.
- Root element: `<aside>`. Not polymorphic — there is no `as` prop. `<aside>` is the semantically correct element for tangentially related content (HTML spec). When nested inside `<article>` or `<section>`, `<aside>` degrades from a `complementary` landmark to a generic container — this is the correct behavior for multiple per-page inline asides (avoids landmark pollution).
- `label` (optional string) — renders a single `<p>` above the body text in the Eyebrow typographic register (`--fs-meta` 14px, `--fg-muted`, `text-transform: uppercase`, `--tracking-eyebrow` 0.06em letter-spacing, `--lh-meta` 1.2 line-height). When absent, no label element is rendered. Default: `undefined`. Example values: `"Note"`, `"Caveat"`, `"Aside"`, `"Data point"`. Do NOT default to `"Note"` — the left rule is the signal; the label is for when type of aside is load-bearing information.
- `children` (required ReactNode) — the body text. Rendered inside a `<p className="body">`. Accepts inline marks only: `<strong>`, `<em>`, `<a>`. No block-level children — no `<h2>`, `<ul>`, `<p>`, `<div>`, `<table>`. Editorial constraint; not type-enforced.
- Left rule: `border-inline-start: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)`. Always present. 1px — quieter than Pull's 3px editorial accent. Decorative; no contrast requirement applies.
- `padding-inline-start: var(--space-3)` (12px) — tighter than Pull's `--space-4` (16px). Reinforces FieldNote's subsidiary character.
- `margin-block: var(--space-6)` (24px) — less than Pull's `--space-8` (32px). FieldNote is a minor inline pause; Pull is a major editorial pause.
- `max-width: var(--hero-max)` (38rem / 608px) — matches the prose column cap used by Pull, Hero, and Section header blocks.
- Body typography: `--fs-body` (17–19px fluid), `--font-sans` (Geist), `font-style: normal`, `line-height: 1.55` (global body value, inline — BACKLOG migration target when `--lh-body` is tokenized), `color: var(--fg)`. FieldNote body is real content, not a caption — full-weight color, not muted.
- Inline links inside `children` inherit the global `<a>` two-layer underline rule from `tokens.css`. At rest: 1px `--hairline` underline. On hover: 1px `--accent` underline grows over it via `background-size` transition (`--dur-mid`, `--easing-link`). On `:focus-visible`: `outline: 2px solid var(--accent)`, `outline-offset: 4px`, `border-radius: var(--radius-1)`. No override in FieldNote's CSS scope — global rule is correct and must NOT be suppressed.
- No states, no motion, no entrance animation. Static typography.
- No color variants (`variant="warning"` etc.) — excluded at the brand level. Colored callouts are a different component. This exclusion requires explicit founder sign-off to change.
- No icon slot. Icons imply status severity; FieldNote is plain text only.
- No multi-paragraph body. Single-paragraph aside. For multiple paragraphs, use Section with `size="tight"`.
- Tokens consumed: `--hairline-w`, `--hairline`, `--space-3`, `--space-1`, `--space-6`, `--fs-body`, `--fs-meta`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--font-sans`, `--tracking-eyebrow`, `--lh-meta`, `--hero-max`. No new tokens introduced.
- Contrast: body `--fg` (#1D1D1F) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) = 16.29:1 (AAA). Label `--fg-muted` (#6E6E73) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) = 4.91:1 (AA normal ✓ at 14px).
- Do NOT use FieldNote for content that has a title, an index, or more than two sentences of structured prose — use `<FailureMode>` or `<Section>`.
- Do NOT nest FieldNote inside `<Pull>`. They operate at different typographic scales.
- Do NOT place FieldNote inside `<Hero>`. Hero owns the page display moment.
- Do NOT pass block-level elements as `children`. No `<h2>`, `<ul>`, `<table>`, `<div>`. Inline marks only.
- Do NOT add an `as` prop. Root is always `<aside>`.
- Do NOT add color variants. Tonally neutral by brand decision.

```tsx
// Plain body — no label (left rule is the signal):
<FieldNote>
  In 2024 we ran the same prompt across three model families and saw consistent
  degradation after context exceeded 16k tokens.
</FieldNote>

// With label — when type of aside is load-bearing:
<FieldNote label="Caveat">
  These numbers reflect Q4 2023 benchmarks. The 2024 evals are in progress
  and results may differ.
</FieldNote>

// With inline link in body:
<FieldNote label="Note">
  The latency figures come from our{" "}
  <a href="/methodology">public methodology doc</a>, updated monthly.
</FieldNote>
```

### Quote
- Attributed customer testimonial molecule. A short prose block (1–4 sentences) from a named person, with optional role/title and optional avatar slot. Primary surface: customer story pages and social-proof sections.
- Root element: `<figure>`. Not polymorphic — `<figure>` is the single correct semantic root for attributed quotations per HTML5. Inside: `<blockquote>` for the quoted body, `<figcaption>` for the attribution row. This structure correctly associates the attribution with the quote at the HTML semantic level — no extra ARIA needed.
- Distinct from `<Pull>` (unattributed editorial pull-quote, Instrument Serif italic, left rule) and `<Statement>` (brand assertion, once per page, no attribution). Quote is sans-serif roman — identifiable at a glance from Pull/Statement without reading the attribution.
- `quote` (required ReactNode) — the quoted body text. Accepts inline marks (`<em>`, `<strong>`) for emphasis within the passage. No block-level children — no headings, lists, nested paragraphs. Rendered as direct child of `<blockquote>`.
- `name` (required string) — the attributed person's name. Plain string; `font-weight: 500`, `--fg`, `--fs-meta` (14px).
- `role` (optional string) — the person's role or title. Plain string. Example: `"VP Engineering, Acme Corp"`. `font-weight: 400`, `--fg-muted`, `--fs-meta`. When omitted, no role element is rendered.
- `avatar` (optional ReactNode) — avatar slot. Accepts any ReactNode: `<img>`, a third-party Avatar, a `<div>` with initials. The DS does not ship an Avatar atom. When present, rendered as the leftmost element of the attribution row; when absent, the name/role column sits flush. Convention: size to 40×40px and clip to circle (`border-radius: 50%`). DS does not enforce sizing or shape. If passing `<img>`, set `alt=""` (decorative) — the `name` is the text alternative.
- Layout: stacked. Body above, attribution row below. No sidebar layout. Stacked degrades cleanly when avatar is absent.
- `<blockquote>` body typography: `--fs-pull` (20–26px fluid), `--font-sans` (Geist), `font-weight: 400`, `font-style: normal`, `line-height: 1.45`, `color: var(--fg)`. `--fs-pull` reuses Pull's scale — both are quoted-content primitives at elevated size. The typographic differentiator is register (sans roman vs. serif italic), not size.
- `<figcaption>` layout: `display: flex`, `align-items: center`, `gap: var(--space-3)` (12px avatar-to-name column). `margin-top: var(--space-4)` (16px) from blockquote. `padding-top: var(--space-4)` (16px) above content. `border-top: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)` — always present; use `className` to suppress if needed.
- Name/role column: `display: flex`, `flex-direction: column`, `gap: var(--space-1)` (4px).
- `max-width: var(--hero-max)` (38rem / 608px) — matches the prose column cap used by Pull, Hero, Section, and FieldNote.
- `margin-block: var(--space-8)` (32px) — matches Pull's block margin. Remove default UA `margin` on `<figure>`.
- `className` merges via `clsx`. `forwardRef` to the `<figure>` root. `displayName = "Quote"`. Full `ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"figure">` spread (id, data-*, aria-*, event handlers).
- No cite prop in v1 — customer story quotes rarely have a URL source; deferred to later revision.
- No showRule prop — hairline rule is always on. Use className override to suppress.
- No avatar shape wrapper — DS does not enforce sizing; consumer responsibility.
- No variant, no size, no as prop.
- Tokens consumed: `--fs-pull`, `--fs-meta`, `--font-sans`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-1`, `--space-3`, `--space-4`, `--space-8`, `--lh-meta`, `--hero-max`. No new tokens introduced.
- Contrast: body/name `--fg` (#1D1D1F) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) = 16.29:1 (AAA). Role `--fg-muted` (#6E6E73) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) = 4.91:1 (AA normal ✓ at 14px). Hairline is decorative.
- Do NOT use Quote for unattributed text — use `<Pull>` if there is no named person.
- Do NOT place Quote inside `<Hero>`. Hero owns the page display moment.
- Do NOT use both Pull and Quote for the same person's words on the same page — pick the register.
- Do NOT render Quote on a `--bg-warm-accent` band — palette interaction is untested.
- Do NOT add a `cite` prop, `showRule` prop, avatar container wrapper, or `as` polymorphism without founder sign-off.
- Do NOT pass block-level elements as `quote`. Inline marks only.

```tsx
// Full Quote — avatar, name, role:
<Quote
  quote="We went from weeks to hours. The tooling handled what we used to staff an entire team for."
  name="Sarah Chen"
  role="VP Engineering, Meridian Labs"
  avatar={
    <img
      src="https://cdn.example.com/avatars/sarah-chen.jpg"
      alt=""
      width={40}
      height={40}
      style={{ borderRadius: "50%", display: "block" }}
    />
  }
/>

// Without avatar:
<Quote
  quote="The accuracy improvements were immediate and measurable."
  name="James Okonkwo"
  role="Head of Data, Fieldwork AI"
/>

// Name only — no role, no avatar:
<Quote quote="Exactly what we needed. Nothing we didn't." name="Priya Mehta" />

// Inline emphasis in quote body:
<Quote
  quote={<>We went from <em>weeks</em> to hours.</>}
  name="Sarah Chen"
  role="VP Engineering, Meridian Labs"
/>
```

### Avatar
- Atom for person/entity identity display. Three modes via discriminated union on `mode` prop.
- `mode="image"` — renders `<img loading="lazy">` with `src` (required) and optional `alt`. `loading` is hardcoded to `"lazy"` — no escape hatch. No `onError` fallback — broken image falls back to browser default; consumers who need a fallback wrap in their own state.
- `mode="initials"` — renders 1–2 character text label inside the avatar shape. Consumer is responsible for the initials string — Avatar does NOT derive initials from a name.
- `mode="empty"` (default when `mode` is omitted) — blank placeholder, no inner content.
- `size` (`"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"md"`) — fixed pixel dimensions: sm=24px, md=32px, lg=40px. These are inline px values in the CSS module (no tokens). Initials font scales proportionally: sm=10px, md=12px, lg=14px.
- `shape` (`"circle"` | `"square"`, default `"circle"`) — circle uses `border-radius: 50%`; square uses `--radius-2` (4px). Inline `border-radius: 50%` in CSS module (no token).
- Hairline ring: `box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px var(--hairline)` — avoids layout shift versus a CSS `border`. Uses `--hairline` token.
- Background: `--surface`. Text color: `--fg-muted`. Font: `--font-sans`, weight 500.
- `name` (optional string) — accessible name for the avatar as a whole. Required in initials and empty modes for a11y. In image mode: required when `alt` is omitted. Produces `role="img"` + `aria-label` on the root `<span>`.
- In image mode with `alt` provided, the `<img>` carries the label and the root span does NOT receive `role="img"` (avoids double-labelling).
- Root is always `<span>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to root span. `...rest` spread to root element.
- Tokens consumed: `--surface` (background), `--fg-muted` (text + initials color), `--hairline` (ring), `--font-sans` (font family), `--radius-2` (square shape).
- Do NOT add `onError` fallback — stateless atom. Consumers own error handling.
- Do NOT pass `loading` — hardcoded `"lazy"` on internal `<img>`. No prop escape hatch.
- Do NOT derive initials from a name string — pass initials explicitly via `initials` prop.
- Do NOT add group/stack composition at this layer — consumer-side concern.
- Do NOT make Avatar polymorphic — root is always `<span>`.
- Do NOT use Avatar for decorative brand imagery — use `<Portrait>` for editorial photography.

### Input
- Single-line text input primitive. Root is always `<input>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to `HTMLInputElement`.
- `type` (`"text"` | `"email"` | `"url"` | `"tel"` | `"password"` | `"search"` | `"number"`, default `"text"`) — constrained to single-line text-like types.
- `invalid` (optional boolean) — when true, sets `data-invalid="true"` and `aria-invalid="true"` on the root element, applies accent border via CSS. Prefer wiring through `<Field error="…">` which also links `aria-describedby`.
- Standard `ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"input">` spread: `value`, `onChange`, `placeholder`, `disabled`, `readOnly`, `required`, `autoComplete`, `aria-*`, `data-*`, etc.
- `clsx` className merge via `className` prop.
- States: default (`--hairline` border, `--bg-elevated` bg), hover (border → `--fg-muted`), focus-visible (2px `--accent` outline + border), invalid (`--accent` border), disabled (`opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed`).
- Error border uses `--accent` — the DS does not ship a danger token in v1. This is the correct brand register.
- Tokens consumed: `--bg-elevated`, `--hairline`, `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--accent`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--radius-2`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`. No new tokens.
- Idiomatic usage wraps Input in `<Field>`. Standalone usage is valid; consumer owns label association.
- Do NOT use Input for multi-line text — use `<Textarea>`.
- Do NOT pass `type="checkbox"`, `type="radio"`, `type="file"`, `type="range"`, `type="date"` — those are not in the allowed type union and require separate primitives.
- Do NOT add prefix/suffix icon slots in v1 — keep the surface minimal.

```tsx
// Inside Field (recommended):
<Field label="Email address" id="email" helper="We'll never share your email.">
  <Input type="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
</Field>

// Standalone (consumer owns label):
<label htmlFor="search">Search</label>
<Input id="search" type="search" />
```

### Textarea
- Multi-line textarea primitive. Root is always `<textarea>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to `HTMLTextAreaElement`.
- `invalid` (optional boolean) — when true, sets `data-invalid="true"` and `aria-invalid="true"`, applies accent border. Prefer wiring through `<Field error="…">`.
- Default `rows={4}`. Consumer can override via `rows` prop.
- `resize: vertical` — horizontal resize disabled. Min-height corresponds to 4 lines of body text.
- Standard `ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"textarea">` spread: `value`, `onChange`, `placeholder`, `rows`, `disabled`, `readOnly`, `required`, `aria-*`, `data-*`, etc.
- `clsx` className merge via `className` prop.
- States: identical to Input — default, hover, focus-visible, invalid, disabled.
- Tokens consumed: `--bg-elevated`, `--hairline`, `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--accent`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--radius-2`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`. No new tokens.
- Do NOT add character count display — consumer responsibility.
- Do NOT use Textarea for single-line input — use `<Input>`.

```tsx
// Inside Field (recommended):
<Field label="Message" id="message">
  <Textarea placeholder="Tell us about your project…" rows={6} />
</Field>
```

### Field
- Composition wrapper that connects a visible label, optional helper text, and optional error message to a single form control (`<Input>`, `<Textarea>`, or any HTML form element).
- Root element: `<div>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to `HTMLDivElement`. `...rest` spread passes `data-*`, `aria-*`, `className`, `style`, etc.
- `label` (required string) — visible label text. Rendered as `<label htmlFor={id}>`. Every Field must have a label.
- `id` (optional string) — auto-generated via `useId()` when not provided. Wires `<label htmlFor>` and the child control's `id`.
- `helper` (optional string) — helper text rendered below the control in `--fg-muted` register. Not rendered when `error` is also set.
- `error` (optional string) — error message rendered below the control with `role="alert"`, `--fg` color, and a 2px `--accent` left-rule border. When set: replaces helper text, injects `invalid={true}` and `aria-invalid="true"` onto the child, includes error element id in `aria-describedby`.
- `required` (optional boolean) — adds visible `*` indicator (in `<span aria-hidden="true">`) next to label, and injects `required` onto child. The `aria-hidden` hides `*` from AT; the `required` attribute conveys it semantically.
- `children` (ReactElement) — exactly one form control. Field clones it via `React.cloneElement` to inject `id`, `aria-describedby`, `aria-invalid`, `invalid`, and `required` as needed.
- `cloneElement` injection table:
  - `id` — always injected (wires label)
  - `aria-describedby` — injected when helper or error is set
  - `aria-invalid="true"` — injected when error is set
  - `invalid={true}` — injected when error is set (for DS Input/Textarea styling)
  - `required={true}` — injected when required is set
- `aria-describedby` points at error element id when error is present; helper element id when only helper is present.
- Error `<p>` has `role="alert"` — announces error message to screen readers on mount.
- Layout: `display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-1)`.
- Label typography: `--fs-meta`, `font-weight: 500`, `--fg`, `cursor: pointer`.
- Helper typography: `--fs-meta`, `--fg-muted`, `--lh-meta`, `margin-top: var(--space-1)`.
- Error typography: `--fs-meta`, `--fg`, `--lh-meta`, `margin-top: var(--space-1)`, `border-inline-start: 2px solid var(--accent)`, `padding-inline-start: var(--space-2)`.
- Tokens consumed: `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--font-sans`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--accent`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`. No new tokens.
- Do NOT add a Form wrapper organism here — that is a separate future organism.
- Do NOT pass multiple children — Field supports exactly one form control.
- Do NOT add inline vs. stacked label variants in v1 — stacked only.
- Do NOT add floating label — not brand-aligned.

```tsx
// (a) Field + Input (email):
<Field label="Email address" id="email" helper="We'll never share your email.">
  <Input type="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
</Field>

// (b) Field + Textarea (message):
<Field label="Message" id="message">
  <Textarea placeholder="Tell us about your project…" />
</Field>

// (c) Field with error:
<Field label="Email address" id="email-err" error="Please enter a valid email address.">
  <Input type="email" defaultValue="not-an-email" />
</Field>

// (d) Required field:
<Field label="Full name" id="name" required>
  <Input placeholder="Arian Zargaran" />
</Field>
```

### Banner
- Persistent inline notice molecule. Sits in document flow; not a toast, not a modal, not a status indicator.
- Root element: `<div>`. `forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, BannerProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Banner"`.
- `tone` (optional `"info" | "warning" | "danger" | "success"`, default `"info"`) — controls color, ARIA role, and left rule.
- `icon` (optional `ReactNode`) — leading icon slot. No auto-icon. Consumer passes decorative SVG with `aria-hidden="true"`.
- `action` (optional `ReactNode`) — trailing action slot. Typically `<Button variant="ghost">`. Natural focus order after body.
- `children` (required `ReactNode`) — body content.
- A11y: `role="status"` (polite live region) for `info` and `success`; `role="alert"` (assertive) for `warning` and `danger`.
- Tone color table:
  - `info`: `--bg` background, `--fg` text, `--hairline-w` / `--hairline` border all sides.
  - `warning`: `--bg-warm-accent` background, `--fg-on-warm` text, 2px `--fg-on-warm-muted` border-inline-start, no full border.
  - `danger`: `--bg-warm-accent` background, `--fg-on-warm` text, 2px `--fg-on-warm-muted` border-inline-start, no full border.
  - `success`: `--bg-elevated` background, `--fg` text, 2px `--accent` border-inline-start, `--hairline-w` / `--hairline` full border.
- Layout: flex row, `align-items: flex-start`, `flex-wrap: wrap`. Icon and action are `flex-shrink: 0`; body is `flex: 1`.
- Tokens consumed: `--bg`, `--bg-elevated`, `--bg-warm-accent`, `--fg`, `--fg-on-warm`, `--fg-on-warm-muted`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--accent`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--space-4`, `--radius-2`. Zero new tokens.
- Static and persistent. No dismissal, no animation, no timing in v1. Consumer controls mount/unmount.
- Toast (ephemeral overlay) is deferred — requires a Provider, queue, and portal.
- Do NOT add auto-icons per tone — icon is always a consumer-controlled slot.
- Do NOT add dismissal logic or animation in v1.
- Do NOT introduce danger/warning/success color tokens — re-use the warm-accent tier.

```tsx
// (a) Info — quiet on-page notice:
<Banner tone="info">Your session will expire in 15 minutes.</Banner>

// (b) Warning — assertive, warm-accent surface:
<Banner tone="warning">API key expires in 3 days. Rotate before the deadline.</Banner>

// (c) Danger — assertive, same warm register as warning:
<Banner tone="danger">Deployment failed. Check the logs and redeploy.</Banner>

// (d) Success — elevated surface, accent left rule:
<Banner tone="success">Deployment complete. Your changes are live.</Banner>

// (e) With icon and action slots:
<Banner
  tone="warning"
  icon={<svg aria-hidden="true">…</svg>}
  action={<Button variant="ghost" size="sm">Rotate key</Button>}
>
  API key expires in 3 days.
</Banner>
```

### SiteShell
- Do NOT nest SiteShell inside another SiteShell. It is min-height: 100dvh.
- Emits plain <a href> tags — framework-router consumers intercept at app level.
- navLabel (default: "Primary") is the aria-label for the nav element.

### Footer
- Canonical site-footer content block. Composes copyright + email (via `<EmailLink variant="muted">`) + optional secondary link row.
- Does NOT own the hairline rule or outer padding when slotted into SiteShell — SiteShell's `.footer` CSS provides those. When used standalone (`as="footer"`), Footer adds `border-top: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)` and `padding: var(--space-8) var(--page-pad)` via the `[data-standalone="true"]` selector.
- `as` prop (`"div"` | `"footer"`, default `"div"`) — controls the root element. Default `"div"` is correct when Footer is the child of SiteShell's `footer` slot (SiteShell already wraps in `<footer>` — nesting two `<footer>` elements degrades the inner one to a generic container per ARIA spec). Pass `as="footer"` for standalone surfaces without SiteShell.
- `copyright` (required string) — full copyright string including `©` and year. e.g. `"© Pouk AI INC 2026"`. DS never auto-inserts `new Date().getFullYear()`. A dev-mode `console.error` fires on empty string.
- `email` (required string) — email address passed to `<EmailLink variant="muted">`. Footer owns no `mailto:` logic.
- `emailLabel` (optional string) — visible label override for EmailLink. When omitted, defaults to the raw email address as visible text (e.g. `hello@pouk.ai`). Pass `"Contact"` for space-constrained contexts.
- `links` (optional `FooterLink[]`) — secondary nav links. Each item: `{ href: string; label: string; external?: boolean }`. When `external: true`, link gets `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`. When absent or empty array, no `<nav>` is emitted.
- `linksLabel` (optional string, default `"Footer"`) — `aria-label` for the `<nav>`. Required for SR landmark disambiguation when multiple `<nav>` elements exist on the page (header nav + footer nav).
- Layout: flex row (`justify-content: space-between`) at ≥ 768px. Below 768px: flex column, left-aligned, `--space-4` (16px) gap between rows.
- Left group: copyright `<span>` + `·` separator (`aria-hidden="true"`) + `<EmailLink variant="muted">`. Gap between items: `--space-2` (8px).
- Right group (optional): `<nav aria-label>` wrapping `<ul>` of `<li><a class="muted-link">` items. Gap between links: `--space-2` (8px). Link color: `--fg-muted` at rest, `--fg` on hover (via `.muted-link` global from `tokens.css`).
- Tokens consumed: `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--space-2`, `--space-4`, `--space-8`, `--page-pad`, `--hairline-w`, `--hairline`, `--dur-fast`, `--accent`, `--radius-1`.
- No new tokens introduced. No dark-mode per-component overrides needed — tokens flip cleanly via the global block in `tokens.css`.
- `className` pass-through available via `React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>` spread. Footer has no margin of its own.
- Do NOT nest Footer inside another Footer.
- Do NOT use `as="footer"` inside SiteShell — this produces nested `<footer>` landmarks, which is semantically invalid. Use the default `as="div"`.
- Do NOT add a router abstraction. Footer emits plain `<a href>`.
- Do NOT pass `external: true` without providing a meaningful label that communicates external destination (e.g. `"GitHub ↗"`).
- Do NOT use Footer for multi-column layout (Products / Company / Legal grid) — that is a separate organism pattern.
- Do NOT add the wordmark inside Footer — use a custom composition outside the DS if needed.
- Accessible name: two `<nav>` on page (header + footer) must each have distinct `aria-label`. Default `"Footer"` satisfies this; override via `linksLabel` if convention differs.

```tsx
// Inside SiteShell (canonical):
<SiteShell
  routes={[{ href: "/why-ai", label: "Why AI" }]}
  footer={
    <Footer
      copyright="© Pouk AI INC 2026"
      email="hello@pouk.ai"
      links={[
        { href: "/privacy", label: "Privacy" },
        { href: "/terms", label: "Terms" },
        { href: "https://github.com/poukai-inc", label: "GitHub ↗", external: true },
      ]}
    />
  }
>
  {children}
</SiteShell>

// Standalone:
<Footer
  as="footer"
  copyright="© Pouk AI INC 2026"
  email="hello@pouk.ai"
  links={[{ href: "/privacy", label: "Privacy" }]}
/>
```

### Dialog

Modal overlay primitive. Always modal (`modal: true`). Wraps `@radix-ui/react-dialog` — all focus-trap, ARIA roles, and keyboard handling are delegated to Radix. The DS adds brand styling only.

**Compound subcomponents**

| Subcomponent        | Role                                                                                              |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Dialog.Root`       | State controller. Props: `open`, `defaultOpen`, `onOpenChange`, `modal` (always true), `children`. |
| `Dialog.Trigger`    | Element that opens the dialog. Use `asChild` to pass a `<Button>`.                               |
| `Dialog.Portal`     | Renders overlay + content outside the DOM tree (into `document.body` by default). See SSR note below. |
| `Dialog.Overlay`    | Backdrop scrim. DS-styled: `rgb(0 0 0 / 0.4)`, animates on `data-state`. Pass `forceMount` for CSS-only exit animations. |
| `Dialog.Content`    | Modal panel. Primary styled surface. Props: `forceMount`, `onOpenAutoFocus`, `onCloseAutoFocus`, `onEscapeKeyDown`, `onPointerDownOutside`, `onInteractOutside`, `className`. |
| `Dialog.Title`      | Required accessible title. Radix warns if absent. Styled at `1.125rem / weight 500` by default. |
| `Dialog.Description`| Optional accessible description. Styled at `--fs-meta` / `--fg-muted`.                          |
| `Dialog.Close`      | Button that closes the dialog. Use `asChild` to render as `<Button>` or icon button.             |

**`DialogBasic` convenience wrapper**

Covers the 90% case: single-panel dialog with title, optional description, body, and built-in X close button.

| Prop           | Type                        | Default   | Description                                                             |
| -------------- | --------------------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `open`         | `boolean?`                  | —         | Controlled open state. Pair with `onOpenChange`.                        |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void?`  | —         | Controlled open state handler.                                          |
| `title`        | `string`                    | required  | Dialog title. Required. Renders as `Dialog.Title`.                      |
| `description`  | `string?`                   | —         | Optional description. When present, adds a hairline rule below title.   |
| `children`     | `ReactNode`                 | required  | Body content.                                                           |
| `footer`       | `ReactNode?`                | —         | Optional right-aligned footer (typically Cancel + Confirm buttons).     |
| `closeLabel`   | `string?`                   | `"Close"` | Accessible label for the built-in X close button. Override for locale.  |
| `className`    | `string?`                   | —         | Forwarded to `Dialog.Content`. Standard layout-override escape hatch.   |

**Tokens consumed**

| Token          | Role                                               |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `--bg-elevated`| Content panel background (front-most elevation)    |
| `--fg`         | Title and body text                                |
| `--fg-muted`   | Description text and close button icon             |
| `--hairline`   | Panel border + title/body divider rule             |
| `--hairline-w` | Hairline stroke weight (1px)                       |
| `--accent`     | Focus ring on close button and interactive elements|
| `--radius-3`   | Content panel corner radius (8px)                  |
| `--radius-1`   | Focus ring border-radius (2px)                     |
| `--space-2`    | Description margin-top                             |
| `--space-3`    | Footer button gap                                  |
| `--space-4`    | Body padding-top; divider margin-top               |
| `--space-6`    | Panel horizontal padding; footer margin-top        |
| `--space-8`    | Panel top padding                                  |
| `--font-sans`  | All text in the dialog                             |
| `--fs-body`    | Body content text                                  |
| `--fs-meta`    | Description text                                   |
| `--dur-fast`   | Open/close transition duration (180ms)             |
| `--easing`     | Entrance easing (expo-out)                         |
| `--page-pad`   | Panel width calculation (subtracted from 100vw)    |
| `--surface`    | Close button hover background                      |

**SSR / portal container note.** By default Radix portals content into `document.body`. Consumers using Next.js App Router or SSR strict mode who need to target a specific DOM node (e.g. `<div id="portal-root">` outside `<main>`) can pass `container` via `Dialog.Portal` props: `<Dialog.Portal container={portalRootRef.current}>`. No code-level handling is required in the DS — the prop passes through to Radix directly.

**JSX usage examples**

```jsx
// (a) Controlled open state — DialogBasic
import { useState } from "react";
import { DialogBasic, Button } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function WaitlistSignup() {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
  return (
    <>
      <Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>Join waitlist</Button>
      <DialogBasic
        open={open}
        onOpenChange={setOpen}
        title="Join the waitlist"
        description="We'll reach out as soon as your spot is ready."
        footer={
          <>
            <Button variant="ghost" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>Cancel</Button>
            <Button variant="primary" type="submit" form="waitlist-form">Submit</Button>
          </>
        }
      >
        <form id="waitlist-form">
          <label htmlFor="email">Email address</label>
          <input id="email" type="email" name="email" autoComplete="email" />
        </form>
      </DialogBasic>
    </>
  );
}

// (b) Uncontrolled via Dialog.Trigger — compound API
import { Dialog, Button } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function InfoOverlay() {
  return (
    <Dialog.Root>
      <Dialog.Trigger asChild>
        <Button variant="ghost">Learn more</Button>
      </Dialog.Trigger>
      <Dialog.Portal>
        <Dialog.Overlay />
        <Dialog.Content>
          <Dialog.Title>About pouk.ai</Dialog.Title>
          <Dialog.Description>
            pouk.ai gives operators access to enterprise-grade AI tooling.
          </Dialog.Description>
          <p>Founded in 2025, pouk.ai builds the infrastructure layer for AI operators.</p>
          <Dialog.Close asChild>
            <Button variant="ghost">Close</Button>
          </Dialog.Close>
        </Dialog.Content>
```
